name: Los Mamellones — Tour from Murcia
region_intro: Hiking around Rio Segura offers diverse landscapes, from the deep Almadenes Canyon carved by the river to the fertile Vega del Segura plain. The region features varied terrain, including pine forests, hills like El Relojero Peak (604 meters), and the clear waters of the Segura River. This area provides a range of natural features for outdoor activities, including numerous hiking trails. Best hiking trails around Rio Segura The most popular hiking route is Veterans 92 Trail (PR-MU 21), an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route is known for its panoramic views and historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote Trail, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. This trail winds through orchards and historic towns, offering scenic views of the agricultural landscape. Local hikers also love the Castillo del Puerto de la Cadena Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. This easy route provides panoramic views of the surrounding area. Hiking around Rio Segura is defined by river canyons, fertile plains, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Rio Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 1200 reviews. More than 6900 hikers have used komoot to explore Rio Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Rio Segura region?
A: The Rio Segura region offers a vast network of nearly 450 hiking trails. These routes cater to all levels, with approximately 130 easy trails, over 240 moderate options, and more than 60 challenging treks for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Rio Segura?
A: The Rio Segura region is incredibly diverse. You can explore deep river canyons like the &lt;strong&gt;Almadenes Canyon&lt;/strong&gt;, fertile plains of the Vega del Segura, serene pine forests, and hills such as &lt;strong&gt;El Relojero Peak&lt;/strong&gt;. The river itself is noted for its remarkably clear waters in its upper and middle sections, and the area is rich in diverse flora and fauna, including various bird species and protected plant life.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Rio Segura?
A: Yes, the Rio Segura region has many family-friendly options. Trails like the &lt;strong&gt;Sendero del Río Segura&lt;/strong&gt; are picturesque and calm, following the river through traditional farms and citrus groves, making them suitable for families. Another easy option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899939794/castillo-del-puerto-de-la-cadena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Puerto de la Cadena Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views over a shorter distance.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Rio Segura region?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900488967/columbares-peak-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Columbares Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate challenge with scenic views. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e893453051/vulture-caves-and-eagle-s-peak-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Caves and Eagle's Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through picturesque areas of the Regional Park El Valle y Carrascoy. For an easier circular walk, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e893422220/the-norias-trail-pr-mu-28-circular-from-abaran" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Norias Trail (PR-MU 28) — Circular from Abarán&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I discover along the trails?
A: Hikers can explore a variety of landmarks. The &lt;strong&gt;Almadenes Canyon&lt;/strong&gt; itself is a major attraction, with an 8-kilometer trail offering views of the Mulata reservoir and access to the Serreta cave with Palaeolithic cave paintings. You can also visit the historically significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope&lt;/a&gt;, nestled within caves along the river. For panoramic vistas, head to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Relojero Peak&lt;/a&gt; or the Mirador Alto de Bayna in Blanca.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Rio Segura?
A: The hiking trails in Rio Segura are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 1,200 reviews. Nearly 7,000 hikers have used komoot to explore the region, often praising the diverse terrain, from river canyons to fertile plains, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some trails offer views of the region's water bodies. The Almadenes Canyon route, for instance, provides views of the Mulata reservoir. Other notable reservoirs in the area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santomera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1553819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mayés Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the broader landscape and can be seen from various routes.

Q: Can I find trails that offer historical insights into the region?
A: Absolutely. The Rio Segura region has a rich history reflected in its trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e892503658/moriscos-huerta-de-ricote-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote Trail&lt;/a&gt; winds through orchards and historic towns, showcasing the agricultural heritage. The long-distance GR 127 (Segura River Trail) highlights ancient irrigation systems and archaeological sites. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e893471664/the-trail-of-the-castles-of-monteagudo-circular-pr-mu-37" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Castles of Monteagudo (Circular PR-MU 37)&lt;/a&gt; offers a journey through historical landscapes.

Q: Are there any trails that feature caves or unique geological formations?
A: Yes, the region is known for its geological interest. The &lt;strong&gt;Almadenes Canyon&lt;/strong&gt; is home to the Serreta cave, which contains significant Palaeolithic cave paintings. The Regional Park El Valle y Carrascoy features intriguing &lt;strong&gt;Vulture Caves&lt;/strong&gt;, which can be explored on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e893453051/vulture-caves-and-eagle-s-peak-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Caves and Eagle's Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Volcanic areas and natural hot springs can also be found along certain routes, adding to the unique landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Rio Segura?
A: While the guide does not specify the best season, the diverse climate of the Rio Segura basin suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant hiking conditions, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be very hot in southeastern Spain, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable for hiking during that period.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in the Rio Segura region?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer adventure, the &lt;strong&gt;GR 127 (Segura River Trail)&lt;/strong&gt; is a significant long-distance route. Spanning 173 kilometers, it showcases spectacular scenery, volcanic landscapes, expansive rice fields, historic waterwheels, and ancient irrigation systems. A particularly challenging section of this route traverses the Almadenes Canyon.
tips: Cresto integral Mamellones. The 1st of 5 peaks. Naturally sculptured by the elements.
